verses.text: Six men come with slaughter weapons, and among them one 'clothed with linen, with a writer's inkhorn.' Judgment and mercy together. God prepares both the sword and the marking pen.
Six men come with slaughter weapons, and among them one 'clothed with linen, with a writer's inkhorn.' Judgment and mercy together. God prepares both the sword and the marking pen.

As the glory rises from the cherub, the man in linen is sent to mark the foreheads of all 'that sigh and... cry for all the abominations.' The mourners are sealed. Those who grieve over sin are known and spared by God.

As the glory rises from the cherub, the man in linen is sent to mark the foreheads of all 'that sigh and... cry for all the abominations.' The mourners are sealed. Those who grieve over sin are known and spared by God.
The rest are sent to slay all without the mark, 'begin at my sanctuary,' starting with the elders. Judgment starts at God's house. The privileged who sinned are first to answer.
The rest are sent to slay all without the mark, 'begin at my sanctuary,' starting with the elders. Judgment starts at God's house. The privileged who sinned are first to answer.
The rest are sent to slay all without the mark, 'begin at my sanctuary,' starting with the elders. Judgment starts at God's house. The privileged who sinned are first to answer.

Ezekiel falls and cries, 'wilt thou destroy all?' but God answers their iniquity is great and 'mine eye shall not spare.' Intercession meets justice. God's mercy is real, yet His holiness will not overlook obstinate sin.

Ezekiel falls and cries, 'wilt thou destroy all?' but God answers their iniquity is great and 'mine eye shall not spare.' Intercession meets justice. God's mercy is real, yet His holiness will not overlook obstinate sin.

Ezekiel falls and cries, 'wilt thou destroy all?' but God answers their iniquity is great and 'mine eye shall not spare.' Intercession meets justice. God's mercy is real, yet His holiness will not overlook obstinate sin.
The man in linen returns: 'I have done as thou hast commanded me.' The work is finished. God's servants carry out His charge exactly, both in saving and in judging.
